matches.match_rationale: Counselors lack structured knowledge of which external providers accept uninsured students or offer sliding-scale fees in the Philippines. A RAG system over a curated provider directory (NGOs, psychiatrists, crisis lines, fee structures) lets counselors query in natural language and receive grounded, citation-backed answers specific to their region and student demographics. This directly addresses the 'counselors don't know which providers accept uninsured students' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A counselor-facing query tool: 'Which providers near Cebu accept uninsured minors for family therapy?' returns ranked providers with fee info, contact details, and intake process — sourced from a maintained directory updated by the school network's welfare team.

matches.match_rationale: Parents drop follow-ups partly due to friction: not knowing how to navigate the referral process, confusion about costs, and stigma-driven avoidance. A conversational agent can guide parents through the referral pathway in a non-judgmental, private channel (SMS or app), answering questions about providers, costs, appointment scheduling, and normalizing help-seeking. Multi-turn dialogue is essential here — a single response doesn't replace the sustained engagement needed to move a hesitant parent to action.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A WhatsApp or SMS-accessible agent (in Filipino/Cebuano) that walks parents through selecting a provider, answers cost/stigma concerns, sends appointment reminders, and collects soft follow-up signals ('Did your child attend?') — reducing dropout from the referral chain.

matches.match_rationale: Follow-up is ad-hoc because there is no workflow: referrals are made verbally or via paper, no system triggers a check-in, and no one owns the tracking loop. A task automation agent can execute the follow-up workflow autonomously — sending check-in messages to parents at set intervals, escalating non-responses to the school counselor, logging outcomes to a case record, and closing the loop when attendance is confirmed. This directly addresses the 'no way to track whether a referred student actually attended' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated post-referral workflow: upon referral entry, the agent schedules parent outreach at Day 3, Day 7, and Day 21; logs responses; flags non-attenders to the counselor dashboard; and generates a monthly referral-completion report for the welfare coordinator.

matches.match_rationale: Matching a student to the right external provider requires weighing multiple factors simultaneously: location, cost, provider specialty, student age, presenting issue (anxiety vs. trauma vs. family conflict), and insurance status. A recommendation system trained on successful past referrals and provider profiles can surface the most contextually appropriate providers for a given student profile — without the counselor needing to manually sift a fragmented directory. This addresses both the 'counselors don't know which providers' mechanism and improves referral quality.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A 'next-best provider' recommender embedded in the counselor's case management tool: given student profile fields (age, issue type, location, insurance), it ranks top 3 external providers with match-score rationale, enabling faster and better-fit warm handoffs.

matches.match_rationale: Parents drop follow-ups for predictable reasons — cost, stigma, prior no-show behavior, travel distance — that can be modeled from student/family attributes available at referral time. A propensity model that scores each referral for 'likelihood of dropout' enables counselors to proactively intensify support (more check-ins, cost-navigation assistance) for high-risk referrals rather than applying uniform follow-up effort to all cases.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A referral dropout risk score generated at the moment of referral, surfaced in the counselor dashboard with the top contributing factors (e.g., 'distance to provider > 10km', 'prior no-show'), triggering a tiered follow-up protocol for high-risk cases.
